statement: photon-limited total SNR is independent of (Delta,P,tau)
required_assumptions: far detuned; weak phase; fixed clean-loss budget; identical independent frames; fixed observable
corrected_wording: Under the idealised far-detuned clean-loss model the photon-limited accumulated SNR is asymptotically independent of detuning, power and exposure.

required_assumptions: noise must change the per-frame detuning exponent; quadratic dark-field observable is the demonstrated case
corrected_wording: Read noise breaks the cancellation when the useful signal falls faster with detuning than the relevant noise, as for read-noise-limited DGI.

statement: total photon-limited SNR is set by the destruction budget
required_assumptions: clean-loss identical-frame model and selected peak-pixel observable
corrected_wording: In the idealised clean-loss model the leading accumulated-SNR scale is set by the destruction budget and observable prefactor.

statement: frames vs per-shot SNR is the SAME for every detuning
required_assumptions: would require identical self-similar evolution and exact asymptotic scaling
corrected_wording: The curves nearly collapse in the ideal scaling model; explicit evolving sequences retain several-percent detuning dependence.

required_assumptions: would require integer heating-plus-reabsorption stopping budget

statement: N_max times tau is fixed by Delta and P
required_assumptions: far-detuned fixed destruction law and fixed reabsorption convention
corrected_wording: N_max tau is approximately fixed at a given detuning and power within the selected far-detuned destruction model.

statement: accumulated SNR is nearly the same along the sweep
required_assumptions: specific sequence settings and RMS estimator
corrected_wording: For this simulated sweep the RMS accumulated SNR varies modestly, retaining an approximate remnant of the ideal invariance.
Photon-limited accumulated SNR is asymptotically invariant in the idealised clean-loss scaling model.

statement: identical N_max applies to every imaging mode
required_assumptions: same incident probe power duration detuning and atomic transition; downstream mode selection
corrected_wording: For identical incident probe conditions the upstream scattering and destruction budget are mode-independent.

statement: PCI and DGI shot-noise curves differ by about two
required_assumptions: current peak-pixel observables ideal opaque stop and carrier-reference PCI
corrected_wording: Under the current peak-pixel definitions PCI has an expected homodyne prefactor near two relative to ideal DGI; this is estimator-dependent.

statement: both ideal curves are approximately detuning-invariant
required_assumptions: caption already states small-phase analytical model and clean-loss budget

statement: linear modes have a fundamental advantage at large detuning
file_location: derived interpretation not explicit in current caption
required_assumptions: would require proof across matched optimal estimators and calibrated noise
corrected_wording: Under the current camera and peak-pixel estimator PCI is more robust to read noise than quadratic DGI at large detuning.
